config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is strongest when individual rights are protected first, because once a government or majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes normal. In my view, the interests of society matter a lot, but they should be pursued by respecting people’s rights, not by putting the group above the person. From my perspective, a fair society is one that protects minorities and dissenters, not just the loudest majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of wording makes me wary, because it can be used to justify trampling free speech, due process, and minority rights whenever a majority claims it is for the greater good. Society does matter, but it is best served when individual rights set the boundaries and the common good is pursued within them. I would only accept limited exceptions, like public safety or emergencies, and even then the burden should be very high.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how easily “for the common good” can become a justification for silencing criticism, pressuring minorities, or excusing weak accountability, so I am cautious of any argument that puts the group first by default. Society does matter, of course, but it is healthier when individual rights are the starting point and any limits are narrow, clear, and truly necessary. If we weaken personal rights too much, the people who suffer first are usually the least powerful, not the ones making the rules.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American point of view, I am especially suspicious of that claim because it can become a blank check for majorities or governments to override speech, privacy, due process, and minority protections in the name of the "greater good." Society is real, but it is healthiest when individual rights set the limits and any exceptions are narrow, specific, and genuinely necessary for things like public safety. If we start by favoring society over the person, the people most likely to pay the price are usually the least powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse bad governance, so I do not trust that phrase when it is used to override personal rights. Society matters, but it is healthier when individual rights come first and any limits are narrow, transparent, and truly necessary. If we reverse that order, the people who suffer most are usually ordinary citizens, not the officials or majorities making the decisions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in the United States, I am very skeptical of any claim that lets a majority or government decide that the “greater good” justifies overriding speech, privacy, due process, or minority protections, because history shows how quickly that can be abused. Society absolutely matters, but it is strongest when individual rights are the starting point and any limits are narrow, transparent, and truly necessary, especially for public safety. If we put society first by default, the people who lose out are usually ordinary citizens, not the people already in power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
